title: Knight Cathedral Tablet Schema
description: Append-only JSONL tablet format for Knight's Cathedral Scribes. Extends Bishop's base format with Knight-specific fields. Instantiated K461/B121.

scope.description: Access scope for this tablet. Values: 'public' (visible to all cross-Cathedral consultation), 'private' (caller-owned only), 'guild:<name>', 'tribe:<name>'. Default for existing tablets: 'public'.

observation.description: What this tablet records — the canonical observation text

category.description: Primary domain tag for routing and retrieval

timestamp.description: ISO 8601 timestamp when this tablet was appended

source_session.description: Session ID that generated this tablet (e.g., K461, B121)

source_document.description: File path or artifact reference this tablet was derived from

bridle_rule_invoked.description: KnightBRIDLEMemory only — which BRIDLE rule was exercised (e.g., 'Rule 2: verify before asserting')

commit_hash.description: Git commit hash if this tablet references a landed commit

git_tag.description: Git tag name if this tablet references a tagged release

k_number.description: Knight session number (e.g., K461) for queue/handoff tablets

supersedes.description: If this tablet corrects a prior one, reference the prior tablet's timestamp here. Never edit in place — always append a correction.

_notes.append_only: Tablets are NEVER modified after writing. If an entry is wrong, append a correction with supersedes pointing to the prior tablet's timestamp. This preserves provenance per A&A #2270 claims.
_notes.idempotency: The Courier uses source_session + source_document as a composite key to detect duplicates. Running twice on unchanged artifacts produces zero new tablets.
_notes.bishop_compat: This schema is additive over Bishop's {ts, session, observation, source, canonical_ref} format. Knight's Scribes use timestamp/source_session/source_document in place of ts/session/canonical_ref for clarity. Cross-Cathedral reads must handle both field-name conventions.
